label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find out the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe we can check them as we go. First, the last day of February is the 29th, right? Because February usually has 28 days, but in a leap year, it has 29. Now, 2000 was a leap year. Wait, how do I know if 2000 is a leap year? The rule for leap years is that they are divisible by 4, but if they're divisible by 100, they must also be divisible by 400. Since 2000 is divisible by 400, it is a leap year. So Jane was born on 2/29/2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. But here's the thing: February 29th only comes every four years. So if today is her 16th birthday, does that mean today is February 29th, 2016? Because from 2000 to 2016 is 16 years, and 2016 is also a leap year. Let me check that. 2016 divided by 4 is 504, no remainder, so yes, it is a leap
